skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate AI images with GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve and 50+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: GPT-Image-2, FLUX Dev LoRA, FLUX.2 Klein LoRA, Gemini 3 Pro Image, Grok Imagine, Seedream 4.5, Reve, ImagineArt. Capabilities: text-to-image, image-to-image, inpainting, LoRA, image editing, upscaling, text rendering. Use for: AI art, product mockups, concept art, social media graphics, marketing visuals, illustrations.
skill.summary: Generate AI images with 50+ models including GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, and Grok via the inference.sh CLI.
skill.author: skills-shell

security_audit.summary: All 55 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is a Markdown documentation file that shows CLI usage examples for the inference.sh belt tool. Backtick patterns flagged as shell execution are actually Markdown code blocks within fenced code blocks. The flagged URLs are legitimate documentation and placeholder example links. No malicious intent or dangerous code patterns were found.

low_findings.title: Markdown code blocks flagged as shell backtick execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 44 Markdown fenced code blocks as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All instances are within ```bash code fences showing CLI usage examples. No actual command execution occurs in the Markdown file. The skill does require Bash access via the allowed-tools directive, but the flagged patterns are documentation, not exploits.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged lines are standard Markdown fenced code blocks. The static analyzer incorrectly interpreted backtick code fences as Ruby shell backtick execution.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs flagged as hardcoded network endpoints
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 10 URLs as hardcoded network references. All URLs point to legitimate documentation (inference.sh), GitHub installation instructions, or are placeholder examples (https://your-image.jpg, https://img1.jpg). No data exfiltration or malicious endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs resolve to the legitimate inference.sh domain or are clearly placeholder examples. No suspicious domains or encoded payloads detected.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm detection is false positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged line 3 for a weak cryptographic algorithm. Line 3 is the YAML front matter description listing model names including Seedream and Gemini. No cryptographic algorithms or operations are present anywhere in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The description field contains model names, not cryptographic algorithms. No crypto-related code exists in the file.

content.user_title: Generate AI Images with 50+ Models via CLI
content.value_statement: Create stunning AI-generated images using 50+ models including GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, and Grok through a single CLI interface. This skill eliminates the need to learn multiple model APIs by providing consistent commands for text-to-image, image editing, upscaling, and inpainting.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using 50+ AI models including GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, and Grok
Edit existing images with inpainting and text rendering capabilities
Upscale images to 4K quality using dedicated upscaling models
Create product mockups and marketing visuals with customizable prompts
Browse and select from a catalog of image generation apps
Stitch multiple images together into a single composite image
limitations: Requires the belt CLI tool to be installed separately
Image quality and generation speed depend on the specific model selected
Cannot train custom models or create custom LoRA weights
Requires an active internet connection for all API calls

use_cases.title: Create Product Visuals for Marketing
use_cases.description: Generate professional product photos with studio lighting using text prompts. Edit backgrounds and apply style changes without a photo studio.

use_cases.title: Generate Concept Art for Projects
use_cases.description: Quickly produce concept art and visual references for design projects. Experiment with different styles and models to find the right look.

use_cases.title: Automate Image Generation in Workflows
use_cases.description: Integrate image generation into automated scripts and development pipelines. Consistent CLI interface works with any automation framework.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of [subject] in [style] using FLUX Dev LoRA
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick image generation with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Product Photography
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a professional product photo of [product], studio lighting, white background, high detail

prompt_templates.title: Image Editing with Inpainting
prompt_templates.prompt: Change the [element] in this image to [new_element] using GPT-Image-2 editing
prompt_templates.scenario: Modifying specific parts of existing images

prompt_templates.title: High Quality Cinematic Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a cinematic portrait of [subject], golden hour lighting, 4K quality using Seedream 4.5

output_examples.input: Generate an image of a cat astronaut in space using FLUX Dev LoRA
output_examples.output: The skill calls the belt CLI with the specified model and prompt. The image is generated by the remote API and saved to the local filesystem. No raw JSON or code output is shown to the user.

output_examples.input: Upscale a product photo to 4K quality using Topaz Upscaler
output_examples.output: The skill processes the image through the upscaling model and returns the enhanced high-resolution version. Original image quality is preserved and improved.

best_practices: Start with FLUX Dev LoRA for high quality results before trying other models
Use specific and detailed prompts for better image quality and accuracy
Keep prompt descriptions under 500 characters for best model compatibility
anti_patterns: Do not use the belt login command inside automated scripts as it requires interactive authentication
Do not assume all models support the same features like inpainting or aspect ratio control
Do not use placeholder image URLs in production workflows without replacing them with actual image paths

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install the belt CLI tool. Run npx skills add belt-sh/cli to install it.

faq.question: Which model should I use for the best quality?
faq.answer: For high quality images, try Seedream 4.5 for 4K cinematic results or GPT-Image-2 for versatile text-to-image and editing tasks.

faq.question: Can I edit existing images with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you can edit images using GPT-Image-2 and P-Image-Edit models. Provide the image URL and describe the changes in your prompt.

faq.question: Is this skill free to use?
faq.answer: The skill itself is free but inference.sh API calls may have usage costs. Check inference.sh for current pricing.

faq.question: Can I generate images without internet access?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires an active internet connection because images are generated through the inference.sh API.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: Format support depends on the specific model. Most models output JPEG or PNG images. Refer to each model documentation for details.
